headline: ‘The fundamentals are proven’: Enervest CEO on building floating solar on a live water utility reservoir
summary_80w: In this interview with PV Tech Premium, Enervest CEO Ross Warby explains the demands of floating solar on a live water utility reservoir.

headline: Lightsource bp, Contact Energy reach financial close on 171MWdc Glorit solar PV plant in New Zealand
summary_80w: Developer Lightsource bp has reached financial close on the 171MWdc Glorit solar PV power plant, north of Auckland, New Zealand.

headline: Australia’s ‘probable’ large-scale renewable energy pipeline surges to 32GW
summary_80w: Australia's large-scale renewables pipeline has reached 32,277MW of probable generation capacity, according to the Clean Energy Regulator.

headline: CEE Roundup: EPCs complete CATL and Sungrow-supplied 600MWh Bulgaria projects
summary_80w: Activity in Central and Eastern Europe (CEE) continues to accelerate, with two separate c.600MWh projects completed in Bulgaria by Solarpro & CATL and Sunotec and Sungrow, plus around 800MWh of capacity progressed across Romania, Estonia, Lithuania, and Finland.

headline: Fire and Rescue NSW mandates 240-minute fire resistance for lithium-ion battery storage rooms in data centres
summary_80w: Fire and Rescue NSW (FRNSW) in Australia has set out its requirements for lithium-ion battery storage rooms and diesel generator structures in data centres.

headline: Neoen Australia signs virtual battery deals for 2.3GWh Western Downs Battery
summary_80w: Neoen Australia has signed virtual battery agreements with energy retailers Nectr and SmartestEnergy for Stages 2 and 3 of the Western Downs Battery in Queensland.

headline: Nextpower expands tracker portfolio with NX Gemini 2P launch
summary_80w: Solar PV solutions provider Nextpower has launched its redesigned NX Gemini two-in-portrait (2P) solar tracker.

headline: DISTRIBUTED SOLAR ROUND-UP: US developers secure funding across Maryland, Delaware and Connecticut
summary_80w: Distributed solar developers including MCEC, Aligned Climate Capital and Catalyst Power have secured funding across US projects.

headline: Alluvial Power brings 150MW Kansas solar project online
summary_80w: Independent power producer (IPP) Alluvial Power has reached commercial operation at its 150MWac project in Ford County, Kansas.

headline: INDIA ROUND-UP: Waaree secures module orders, Bondada wins EPC contract and Navitas plans cell plant
summary_80w: Navitas announced investment, Bondada secured EPC contract, SolarSquare raised US$53 million, Gujarat Inject and Waaree won module orders.

headline: Fonterra signs virtual PPA with NZ Clean Energy for 129MW Darfield solar-plus-storage project in New Zealand
summary_80w: Foresight Group-backed developer NZ Clean Energy (NZCE) and Fonterra have signed a long-term virtual power purchase agreement (PPA) under which the dairy cooperative will purchase electricity generated by NZCE's Darfield solar-plus-storage project in Canterbury.

headline: Limited synergies and profitability drive Wärtsilä’s energy storage divestment
summary_80w: Wärtsilä spinning out its energy storage activities and divesting a 50% stake this week reflects difficulties in making the business profitable and synergistic within the wider group activities, analysts said.

headline: EU Roundup: Octopus and Eco Stor financing deals for large-scale BESS in Italy and Germany enable 1.6GWh of capacity
summary_80w: Octopus Energy Generation has partnered with ZE Energy to invest in and build one of Italy's biggest BESS projects, while Eco Stor has secured financing for one of Germany's, which it is already building. Optimiser Entrix has also won two large contracts in Germany and the Netherlands.

headline: Solar Energy Corporation of India invites bids for 4.8GWh of energy storage-backed FDRE capacity
summary_80w: SECI is inviting bids for 4,800MWh of firm and dispatchable renewable energy (FDRE) capacity supported by co-located energy storage.

headline: 14.6GWh of BESS deployed globally in May; activity up 24% in 2026 so far
summary_80w: Around 4.9GW/14.6GWh of large-scale BESS came online in May, with a much more balanced regional breakdown than usual.

headline: Southeast Asia’s BESS developers must ‘engage early with regulators, utilities and communities’
summary_80w: Developers of battery storage projects across Southeast Asia must heed lessons learned elsewhere and engage with key stakeholders as early as possible, Energy-Storage.news has heard.

headline: US Roundup: Virginia’s biggest standalone BESS, Cypress Creek raises US$3.5 billion and Kore sells mobile battery business
summary_80w: Virginia’s biggest standalone BESS comes online, Cypress Creek raises funding for a gigawatt-scale hybrid resource, and Kore Power’s mobile solutions subsidiary is sold, in this edition of news in brief.

headline: 5GW Bogunda wind project in Australia reconfigured as 1.85GW hybrid with 500MW solar and 2GWh of battery capacity
summary_80w: Brisbane-based developer Renewable Energy Partners (REP) has expanded the Bogunda Energy Hub to include solar PV and battery energy storage, with the project now formally in early-stage development south of Hughenden in Queensland, Australia.

summary_80w: The NSW Energy Security Corporation has deployed its first investment, committing AU$100 million to a large-scale battery storage platform.

headline: European inverter manufacturing capacity surpasses 100GW

headline: US solar developers are getting creative—and risky—ahead of the safe harbour deadline

headline: MPC begins testing and commissioning work at 66.1MW Guatemala solar PV project

headline: Ember: Spain’s replacement of gas with renewable energy has delivered bill savings of 19%
summary_80w: The deployment of renewable energy capacity in Spain has driven a decoupling from volatile global gas prices, resulting in a 19% reduction in consumer electricity bills.

headline: ‘Everyone’s too enamoured with low price to the detriment of what they’re going to get for that’
summary_80w: PV Talk: Todd Heffner explains what are the most common issues he's dealt in solar litigations, as well as the importance of finding the right experts.

summary_80w: AESC has announced a strategic supply partnership with BESS system integrator Prevalon Energy.

summary_80w: SECI is inviting bids for 4,800MWh of firm and dispatchable renewable energy capacity supported by co-located energy storage systems.

headline: Enphase launches IQ9N microinverter for European residential solar

headline: CPUC finalises community solar scheme, CLASS argues programme is ‘destined for continued failure’
summary_80w: CPUC has finalised details of its community solar, which has been dismissed as 'unworkable and destined for continued failure' by CLASS.

headline: SmartHelio bags investment to develop AI agent for solar and BESS operations

headline: Contact Energy completes solar module installation at 150MW solar PV power plant in New Zealand

headline: Inside South Australia’s FERM tender: Why a technology-neutral process produced an all-battery outcome

headline: APAC data centres risk a fossil fuel dependency long-duration energy storage can help end

headline: Rooftop solar momentum builds in India but growth remains uneven
